actionLibraryList.title: Mat Lying Toe Taps
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on your back with arms by your sides. Bend the hips and knees to tabletop position (90°), keeping a neutral spine and core engaged. Inhale to prepare.
2.Exhale, engage the abdominals, maintain pelvic stability, and lower one leg in a controlled manner, lightly tapping the toes on the floor without moving the torso.
3.Inhale, return the leg to tabletop position.
4.Exhale, switch to the other leg and repeat the toe tap, alternating sides. Maintain core stability and controlled rhythm throughout the movement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770688634189.jpg","context":"1.Keep the lower back stable, pressing the sacrum into the mat.\n2.Relax the shoulders and neck, allowing them to sink downward."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to engage and lower the leg.
Inhale to return with control.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid arching or sagging the lower back.
2.Do not lower the legs too quickly or use momentum to tap the toes.
3.Keep the shoulders relaxed; avoid shrugging.

actionLibraryList.title: Mat Scapula Isolation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on your back with a neutral spine and pelvis. Place your feet hip-width apart on the mat. Extend your arms straight toward the ceiling with palms facing inward. Keep the shoulder blades neutral.
2.Inhale, protract the shoulder blades, creating a stretch between them.
3.Exhale, return the shoulder blades to neutral, extending and stretching the posterior shoulder girdle.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770688675769.jpg","context":"1.Keep the lower back stable, pressing the sacrum into the mat.\n2.Keep the lower limbs firmly planted and still."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to return the shoulder blades to neutral.
Inhale to protract and stretch between the shoulder blades.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid over-retracting the shoulder blades during the movement.
2.Keep the spine stable; avoid any spinal movement.

actionLibraryList.title: Mat Arm Circles
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on your back with a neutral spine and pelvis. Place your feet hip-width apart on the mat. Extend your arms along your sides with palms facing down.
2.Inhale, lift both arms overhead in a controlled arc. Limit the range so that the abdominals remain engaged, the shoulder blades stay stable, and the lower ribs stay in contact with the mat.
3.Exhale, sweep the arms in an arc back down along the sides to the hips. Repeat the movement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770688692658.jpg","context":"1.Keep the lower back stable, pressing the sacrum into the mat.\n2.Keep the shoulders and neck relaxed, with the shoulders down."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Inhale to lift and extend the arms overhead.
Exhale to sweep the arms back down in a controlled arc.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid flaring the ribs or arching the lower back; maintain core control.
2.Keep the shoulders relaxed and shoulder blades stable; avoid shrugging.

actionLibraryList.title: Lying Straight Down
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale, brace your core, keep the arms straight, and drive the movement from the back as you press both arms down along your sides toward the outer thighs.
2.As the arms press down, keep the pelvis and spine stable. Maintain the tabletop position—do not let the legs shift or rock with the arm movement.
3.Inhale and, resisting the cable tension, slowly return the arms to the start with control, maintaining constant tension.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770626631633.jpg","context":"1.Bend the knees and hips to lift both legs into a tabletop position, with the knees stacked directly over the hips.\n2.Gently press the lower back into the mat and keep the pelvis stable.\n3.Relax the shoulders and let them drop away from the ears."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale as you press the arms straight down.
Inhale as you slowly return to the start.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the arms, shoulders, back, and core.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Do not let the legs move or pull the knees toward the chest.
2.Keep the lower back in contact with the mat and avoid rib flare.
3.Keep the elbows straight—do not bend the arms.
4.Do not shrug the shoulders or grip with the wrists.

actionLibraryList.title: Lying Side Arm
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale and engage the back muscles as you smoothly lower the arms from the sides down toward the outer thighs.
2.As you pull down, draw the shoulder blades toward the spine and down—avoid shrugging or forcing with the shoulders.
3.Inhale and resist the tension as you slowly return the arms to the T-position, emphasizing control on the return.
4.Throughout, keep the legs in tabletop, and maintain a stable pelvis and spine.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770626672042.jpg","context":"1.Position the knees directly above the hips in a stable tabletop.\n2.Press the lower back into the mat and keep the pelvis stable.\n3.Relax the shoulders and draw them down away from the ears."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to pull the arms down.
Inhale to slowly return the arms.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the shoulders, back, and arms.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the ribs neutral—avoid flaring or collapsing the lower back.
2.Do not shrug the shoulders.
3.Keep the wrists straight—avoid bending them.
4.Keep the knees away from the chest.

actionLibraryList.title: Lying Arm Circles
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Open the arms out to the sides, lower them close to the floor, then press down. From near the hips, control the arms inward and upward, drawing circles along the sides of the body, and finish with the arms returning to a position above the chest.
3.Keep the arms below shoulder level throughout the movement.
4.Maintain a stable tabletop position with the legs, keeping the pelvis and spine steady the entire time.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770626713742.jpg","context":"1.Keep the knees stacked directly over the hips, maintaining a stable tabletop position.\n2.Gently press the lower back into the mat and keep the pelvis stable.\n3.Relax the shoulders and draw them down away from the ears."}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the back, core, and arms.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the ribs from flaring and avoid arching the lower back.
2.Avoid shrugging the shoulders.
3.Keep the wrists neutral—don’t flex them.
4.Don’t let the knees drift toward the chest.

actionLibraryList.title: Side-Kneeling External Rotation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale and engage your core. Initiate shoulder strength to pull the handle from the front-diagonal to the back-diagonal, keeping your upper arm close to your body.
2.Inhale and maintain a stable torso. Slowly return the handle to the starting position, controlling the movement eccentrically against the cable.
3.Keep your pelvis facing forward throughout, avoiding rotation or side-tilt from one-sided effort.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770627983320.jpg","context":"1.Keep your shoulders down.\n2.Keep your upper arms close to your body.\n3.Maintain a neutral pelvis."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to exert force.
Inhale to slowly return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid compensating with your lower back.
2.Keep your arms close to your body.
3.Maintain your center of gravity.

actionLibraryList.title: Side-Kneeling Internal Rotation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale and engage your arm and shoulder to pull the same-side arm diagonally forward to the opposite rear-diagonal.
2.Keep your torso facing forward throughout, and maintain a tight core to prevent rotation or side-tilt.
3.Inhale and slowly return to the starting position, controlling the movement eccentrically against the cable.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770628006654.jpg","context":"1. Keep the shoulders down (avoid shrugging).\n2. Keep the upper arms glued to the sides of the body throughout.\n3. Keep the pelvis stable (no rocking or shifting)."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to exert force.
Inhale to return slowly.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid compensating with your lower back.
2.Keep your arms close to your body.
3.Maintain your center of gravity.

actionLibraryList.title: Draw a Sword
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale and engage your arms and shoulders to lift your arm diagonally upward along the opposite diagonal.
2.Keep your torso facing forward and maintain a tight core throughout to prevent rotation or side-tilt.
3.Inhale and eccentrically control the handle back to the starting position, resisting the cable tension and avoiding a rapid pull.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770628025775.jpg","context":"1.Keep your shoulders down.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine.\n3.Keep your pelvis stable."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to lift your arm diagonally upward.
Inhale to slowly return the handle.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep your shoulders down and neck relaxed.
2.Keep your torso stable, avoiding rotation or sagging.

actionLibraryList.title: Side-Kneeling External Rotation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale and engage your core. Initiate shoulder strength to pull the handle from the front-diagonal to the back-diagonal, keeping your upper arm close to your body.
2.Inhale and maintain a stable torso. Slowly return the handle to the starting position, controlling the movement eccentrically against the cable.
3.Keep your pelvis facing forward throughout, avoiding rotation or side-tilt from one-sided effort.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770627983320.jpg","context":"1.Keep your shoulders down.\n2.Keep your upper arms close to your body.\n3.Maintain a neutral pelvis."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to exert force.
Inhale to slowly return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid compensating with your lower back.
2.Keep your arms close to your body.
3.Maintain your center of gravity.

actionLibraryList.title: Side-Kneeling Internal Rotation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale and engage your arm and shoulder to pull the same-side arm diagonally forward to the opposite rear-diagonal.
2.Keep your torso facing forward throughout, and maintain a tight core to prevent rotation or side-tilt.
3.Inhale and slowly return to the starting position, controlling the movement eccentrically against the cable.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770628006654.jpg","context":"1. Keep the shoulders down (avoid shrugging).\n2. Keep the upper arms glued to the sides of the body throughout.\n3. Keep the pelvis stable (no rocking or shifting)."}]

Exhale to exert force.
Inhale to return slowly.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid compensating with your lower back.
2.Keep your arms close to your body.
3.Maintain your center of gravity.

actionLibraryList.title: Draw a Sword
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale and engage your arms and shoulders to lift your arm diagonally upward along the opposite diagonal.
2.Keep your torso facing forward and maintain a tight core throughout to prevent rotation or side-tilt.
3.Inhale and eccentrically control the handle back to the starting position, resisting the cable tension and avoiding a rapid pull.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770628025775.jpg","context":"1.Keep your shoulders down.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine.\n3.Keep your pelvis stable."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to lift your arm diagonally upward.
Inhale to slowly return the handle.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep your shoulders down and neck relaxed.
2.Keep your torso stable, avoiding rotation or sagging.

actionLibraryList.title: Chest Expansion with Head Rotation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Inhale. Engage the back and rear shoulder muscles to pull the arms backward slightly past the sides of the body and hold.
2.Hold your breath. Rotate the cervical spine to the right, then turn the head to the left, and return to neutral.
3.Exhale. Slowly and under control, bring the hands forward to return the handles to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770628932212.jpg","context":"1.Keep the torso stable.\n2.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down.\n3.Keep the arms straight."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Inhale on effort.
Exhale to return.
Hold breath for cervical rotation.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the wrists neutral; avoid bending.
2.Keep the shoulders down and relaxed.
3.Maintain a stable pelvis; avoid sagging the lower back.

actionLibraryList.title: Chest Expansion Row
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale. Engage the back and rear shoulder muscles, bending the elbows and pulling them vertically back to about 90°.
2.Inhale. Maintain tension in the back and slowly extend the elbows forward, returning the handles to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770630463366.jpg","context":"1.Keep the torso stable.\n2.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Inhale on effort.
Exhale to return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the pelvis and lower body stable; avoid sagging the lower back.
2.Keep the shoulders down and relaxed.
3.Return slowly; avoid releasing too quickly.

actionLibraryList.title: Chest Expansion Row Rotation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale. Bend the right elbow and rotate the thoracic spine to the right, keeping the left arm extended.
2.Inhale. Slowly extend the right arm, controlling the return.
3.Switch sides. Exhale, bend the left elbow and rotate the thoracic spine to the left, keeping the right arm extended. Inhale, slowly extend the left arm back with control.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770631344263.jpg","context":"1.Keep the torso stable.\n2.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Inhale on effort.
Exhale to return.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the shoulders, back, and arms.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the pelvis and lower body stable; avoid sagging the lower back.
2.Keep the shoulders down and relaxed.
3.Return slowly; avoid releasing too quickly.

actionLibraryList.title: Shoulder Stretch
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lightly engage the core, keep the pelvis neutral and stable, and let the shoulders relax down.
2.Inhale to prepare. Extend one arm straight across the chest, keeping the palm relaxed and the shoulder blade stable without shrugging. Use the opposite forearm or hand to gently press the arm closer to the chest, feeling a gentle stretch across the rear shoulder, posterior deltoid, and surrounding shoulder joint. Breathe evenly while holding the stretch.
3.Exhale slowly to release back to neutral, then repeat on the other side.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770691152685.jpg","context":"1.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine and stable pelvis."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Breathe evenly while holding the stretch.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel a stretch around the shoulder joint.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down.
2.Draw the ribs in, avoid flaring, and keep the torso aligned without twisting.

actionLibraryList.title: Triceps Stretch
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Sit with a long spine, lightly engage the core, keep the pelvis neutral and stable, relax the shoulders, and release the neck.
2.Inhale to prepare. Raise one arm, bending the elbow to bring the hand toward the upper back. Use the other hand to gently support the raised elbow, applying a slight inward and backward pressure to lengthen the triceps. Maintain even breathing and feel a gentle stretch along the back of the upper arm.
3.Exhale and slowly release back to the starting position. Repeat on the other side.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770691135077.jpg","context":"1.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine with a stable pelvis."}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel the stretch along the back of the upper arm (triceps).
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid shrugging the shoulders.
2.Keep the ribs drawn in, maintaining a stable lower back without excessive arching.

actionLibraryList.title: Chest Stretch
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lightly engage the core, keep the pelvis neutral and stable, lengthen the spine, and let the shoulders relax down.
2.Inhale to prepare. With both arms straight, stack them in front of the body to one side, keeping the elbows extended and the shoulder blades stable. Exhale as the rib cage leads the upper body into an open rotation to the opposite side, reaching the top arm back behind the body. Lift and open the sternum to feel a gentle stretch across the front of the chest and shoulders. Breathe evenly.
3.Exhale with control to return to neutral and repeat on the other side.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770691265549.jpg","context":"1.Keep your shoulders relaxed and down.\n2.Keep the pelvis stable."}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel a stretch across the front of the chest and shoulders.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid shrugging the shoulders.
2.Keep the pelvis neutral and level.
